tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":57926,"title":"[AgentServer] Docs: Document new ResponseEventStream convenience generators in README and CHANGELOG","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` **Triggered by:** PR #57895 by `@RaviPidaparthi` ## What Changed PR #57895 added new **convenience generators** on `ResponseEventStream` — a middle ground between `TextResponse` (simple text-only) and the full builder API (`AddOutputItemMessage()` etc.): - `stream.OutputItemMessage(string text)` — emits a complete text message (all inner events) in a single call - `stream.OutputItemMessage(IAsyncEnumerable tokens, CancellationToken)` — streams token-by-token with each chunk becoming a `response.output_text.delta` event - `stream.OutputItemFunctionCall(name, callId, arguments)` — emits a complete function call output item in one call - `stream.OutputItemReasoningItem(...)` — emits a reasoning output item The samples (Sample 3, 4, 6) were restructured to lead with convenience-first patterns, but the **main README** and **CHANGELOG** were not updated to reflect these new APIs. ## Gaps Found - The main `README.md` "Key concepts" section documents only `TextResponse` and the full builder control pattern (`AddOutputItemMessage()` / `AddTextContent()` / `EmitAdded()` etc.) — the convenience generators (`stream.OutputItemMessage()`, `stream.OutputItemFunctionCall()`, `stream.OutputItemReasoningItem()`) are invisible to a developer reading only the README - The CHANGELOG (`CHANGELOG.md`) has only the initial release entry and does not mention any of the convenience generators added in PR #57895 - The README description of `ResponseEventStream` should clarify when to use convenience generators vs. the full builder API (currently missing entirely from the key concepts section) 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — EDIT In the **Key concepts → ResponseHandler** section, after the existing `ResponseEventStream — full control over every SSE event:` subsection, add a new subsection documenting convenience generators. Replace the current `ResponseEventStream` description block (approximately lines 57–81) with an expanded version: ```markdown **`ResponseEventStream` — convenience generators (recommended for most cases):** When `TextResponse` is too simple but the full builder API is more than you need, use the convenience generators on `ResponseEventStream`. They handle all inner events (`output_item.added`, `content_part.added`, deltas, `content_part.done`, `output_item.done`) automatically: ``` public class GreetingHandler : ResponseHandler { public override async IAsyncEnumerable CreateAsync( CreateResponse request, ResponseContext context, [EnumeratorCancellation] CancellationToken cancellationToken) { await Task.CompletedTask; var stream = new ResponseEventStream(context, request); yield return stream.EmitCreated(); yield return stream.EmitInProgress(); // One call emits all text output events automatically. var input = request.GetInputText(); foreach (var evt in stream.OutputItemMessage($"Hello! You said: \"{input}\"")) yield return evt; yield return stream.EmitCompleted(); } } ``` For token-by-token streaming, pass an `IAsyncEnumerable`: ``` await foreach (var evt in stream.OutputItemMessage(tokenSourceAsync, cancellationToken)) yield return evt; ``` For function calling, use `OutputItemFunctionCall()`: ``` foreach (var evt in stream.OutputItemFunctionCall("get_weather", "call_1", arguments)) yield return evt; ``` **`ResponseEventStream` — full builder control:** Use the builder API only when you need to interleave non-event work *between individual delta/done calls within a content part*, or set custom properties on an output item before `EmitAdded()`: ``` // ... (existing code) ... ``` ``` #### File 2: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/CHANGELOG.md` — EDIT Add a new entry under `## 1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` in `### Features Added`: ```markdown - Added `ResponseEventStream` convenience generators for emitting complete output items in one call: - `OutputItemMessage(string text)` — emits a full text message output item (handles all inner SSE events automatically) - `OutputItemMessage(IAsyncEnumerable tokens, CancellationToken)` — streams tokens as `response.output_text.delta` events - `OutputItemFunctionCall(name, callId, arguments)` — emits a complete function call output item - `OutputItemReasoningItem(...)` — emits a reasoning output item - Samples restructured for progressive complexity: convenience-first patterns in Samples 3, 4, and 6 with full builder variants for advanced use cases. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The convenience generator snippets referenced in Step 1 need corresponding `#region` / `#endregion` blocks in a sample test file. 1. **Write the sample code** in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Samples/Sample0_ReadMe.cs` (create if it doesn't exist, or add to an existing ReadMe sample file) For each snippet (`Responses_ReadMe_ConvenienceHandler`, `Responses_ReadMe_StreamingConvenienceHandler`, `Responses_ReadMe_FunctionCallConvenience`), wrap the handler class or usage snippet with: ```csharp #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ConvenienceHandler // ... handler code ... #endregion ``` 2. **Add snippet placeholders in README.md** at the exact locations described in Step 1: ````markdown ```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ConvenienceHandler ``` ```` 3. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` to inject the actual code. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already has all required sections: 1. ✅ Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Configure the server) 2. ✅ Key concepts — **INCOMPLETE**: missing convenience generator documentation 3. ✅ Examples (links to samples) 4. ✅ Troubleshooting 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing Only the **Key concepts** section needs updating as described in Step 1. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — injects snippet code into README placeholders; check `git diff` to confirm README updated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — regenerates public API listing if any public types changed 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24126305845/agentic_workflow) · ● 616.3K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57926","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-08T08:52:01Z","updated_at":"2026-04-08T08:52:01Z"}

arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue documents a documentation gap in the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` package — the README and CHANGELOG were not updated after PR #57895 added new `ResponseEventStream` convenience generators for emitting output items (text messages, function calls, reasoning items).

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` (1.0.0-beta.1, unreleased)
**Affected API:** `ResponseEventStream` — convenience generator methods (`OutputItemMessage`, `OutputItemFunctionCall`, `OutputItemReasoningItem`)
**Scenarios:**
- Developer reads README "Key concepts" section and cannot discover the convenience generators; only `TextResponse` and the full builder API (`AddOutputItemMessage()` / `AddTextContent()` / `EmitAdded()`) are documented
- CHANGELOG has no entry for the convenience generators added in PR #57895
- No guidance in README on when to choose convenience generators vs. the full builder API

**Root ask:** Update `README.md` (Key concepts → ResponseHandler section) to document the new convenience generators, and add a CHANGELOG entry under `## 1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)`.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation gap issue (not a code bug). The relevant files are:
- `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md`
- `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/CHANGELOG.md`

The issue body includes a detailed implementation guide with exact file edits, snippet region names, and validation commands.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review PR #57895 to confirm which convenience generator methods were added and their signatures
2. Update the `README.md` Key concepts section with convenience generator examples as described in the implementation guide
3. Add a CHANGELOG entry under `## 1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` listing the new APIs
4. If code snippets are added to the README, run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` to inject them from sample files
5. Build and test: `dotnet build` + `dotnet test --filter TestCategory!=Live` to confirm nothing is broken
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` is a data-plane client SDK (not a management or provisioning package); the issue is about SDK documentation
- **Service:** `AI Agents` — the package lives in `sdk/agentserver/` which maps to the `%AI Agents` ServiceLabel in CODEOWNERS (line 85)
- **Confidence:** High — the issue body explicitly names the service directory (`sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`), there is no ambiguity about service or category, and the CODEOWNERS mapping is direct
</details>
